data.description:
## Finding (DE5 / F14 — verified; dormant machinery)
`verify_attested_commit` shells out to `git verify-commit`/`git log` inheriting the caller CWD, so verification fails-closed when run from a sibling worktree rather than the attestation repo. Investigation: the function has NO production callers today (only tests) — it is part of an unwired band-attestation gate. So fixing in isolation adds an untestable param, but the maintainer approved a minimal, back-compat-safe fix now.

Citations: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_attestation.py:91-135` (TODO(bug TBD — F14) at :94).

## Reproduction Steps
1. Attest a commit; run `verify_attested_commit(sha)` from a sibling worktree (different CWD).
2. Expected: verifies against the attestation repo. Actual: git subprocess can't find the sha -> returns False (fail-closed) spuriously.

## Remediation
Add an optional `repo_root: Path | None = None` param; when set, pass `-C <repo_root>` to both git subprocesses. Default preserves today's behavior (no callers to break). Fold into the git-adapter ticket's explicit-repo_root seam if that lands first.

cascade_flag: adds an optional param to `verify_attested_commit` — check the 4 test call sites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `verify_attested_commit` accepts an explicit repo root and passes `-C` to git
- [ ] Default (no arg) behavior unchanged
- [ ] A test exercises the sibling-worktree/CWD case
data.title: Fix attestation git verify CWD bug (F14): explicit repo root / -C
